audit_items.proof_trace: The traversal in json_value_free_ex reads `value->parent` (L991) before calling `mem_free(cur_value, ...)` (L992) at every exit point, and accesses `values[n]` entries only under the `length != 0` branch while freeing `values` only under the mutually exclusive `length == 0` branch — so no pointer is ever dereferenced after being freed. Each node is reachable via exactly one parent pointer (ensured by the parser's tree construction) and is freed exactly once when backtracking through it with length == 0, so no double-free is possible.
